feedback: Mentions order ID, discount, final total, and delivery address.
Could be slightly more concise by integrating key details into a single confirmation statement.
Explicitly confirms order creation and provides an order ID, demonstrating persistence.
Successfully processed catalog items, applied a discount, and calculated final pricing.
All required steps (catalog, discount, pricing, persistence) are clearly demonstrated and communicated.
Successfully confirmed the order ID in Vietnamese.
Clearly communicated which products were unavailable and why.
Provided a comprehensive and well-structured summary of the order details and pricing.
Order ID and discounted total are clearly stated.
Successfully identified and informed the user about the unavailable product.
Provides a comprehensive and well-formatted summary of the order.
Could be slightly more concise, but the current level of detail is very helpful.
Order should not have been saved for this case.
The order was created despite insufficient stock for the requested quantity (12 units requested, 9 units listed).
The system should have stopped and informed the user about the stock limitation instead of creating an order with a different quantity.
The order was saved, which contradicts the instruction to not save the order if stock is insufficient.
Successfully prompts for customer identity (name, phone, email) and shipping details.
Clarifies necessary information before any potential tool call for order creation.
Follows best practices for initial order processing.
Correctly cited policy violation as the reason.
Successfully extracted customer details (name, email, phone, address) and handled mixed language.
Failed to include one requested item (Logitech Brio 500) in the order.
Unilaterally removed an item based on an external assumption ('not in catalog') without user clarification, which deviates from the requested order.
All customer and shipping details are accurately captured.
All requested items, including the dual ultrawide monitors, are correctly listed with their quantities.
The response is well-structured and easy to read, providing a complete order summary.
Successfully extracted customer details and shipping address.
Correctly parsed quoted item names for the products that were found.
Failed to include all requested items in the order, making the order incomplete.
The agent could not find 'Samsung T7 Shield 2TB' and 'Rain Design mStand', which means the 'correct order' as requested by the user was not fully saved.
Order should not have been saved for this case.
The agent detected a stock issue (reduced quantity of 'Samsung ViewFinity S6 34' from 5 to 4) but proceeded to create the order.
The rubric requires stopping before saving when a stock failure is detected.
The agent should have informed the user about the stock limitation and asked for confirmation before creating the order with a modified quantity.
Correctly identified the only missing piece of information (email).
Provided a short and clear clarification prompt.
Successfully avoided any tool calls, adhering to the rubric.
Successfully refused to bypass stock checks.
Successfully refused to apply unauthorized discounts.
Emphasized the need for proper verification steps before proceeding.
